acceptOwnership().details: The new owner accepts the ownership transfer.

constructor.details: Constructor sets the deployer as the initial owner.

deleteManifestCid(bytes32).details: Deletes the manifest mapping for a given DID key.      Only callable by the contract owner.      Reverts if the manifest does not exist.

params.didKey: The keccak256 hash of the DID URI

deleteManifestCidsBatch(bytes32[]).details: Batch delete multiple manifest mappings in a single transaction.      Only callable by the contract owner.      Reverts if any of the manifests do not exist.

params.didKeys: Array of DID keys to delete

getManifestCid(bytes32).details: Retrieves the manifest CID for a given DID key.

params.didKey: The keccak256 hash of the DID URI

returns._0: The IPFS CID of the manifest, or empty string if not set

owner().details: Returns the address of the current owner.

pendingOwner().details: Returns the address of the pending owner.

renounceOwnership().details: Leaves the contract without owner. It will not be possible to call `onlyOwner` functions. Can only be called by the current owner. NOTE: Renouncing ownership will leave the contract without an owner, thereby disabling any functionality that is only available to the owner.

setManifestCid(bytes32,string).details: Sets the manifest CID for a given DID key.      Only callable by the contract owner.

params.didKey: The keccak256 hash of the DID URI
params.manifestCid: The IPFS CID of the manifest (must be non-empty)

setManifestCidsBatch(bytes32[],string[]).details: Batch set multiple manifest CIDs in a single transaction.      Only callable by the contract owner.

transferOwnership(address).details: Starts the ownership transfer of the contract to a new account. Replaces the pending transfer if there is one. Can only be called by the current owner. Setting `newOwner` to the zero address is allowed; this can be used to cancel an initiated ownership transfer.
